description: Red Lion Crimson 3.0 is open. You are configuring a three-element boiler drum level control system for a 150 MW coal-fired power plant. Two reference documents are open in Notepad on the Desktop: boiler_instrument_schedule.csv (tag register with types, ranges, units, and alarm limits) and asme_ptc4_control_narrative.txt (pressure compensation formulas, control algorithm, data logging requirements, and display layout). Create the ACTIVE sensor tags from the instrument schedule (LT_100, FT_100, FT_101, PT_100, CV_100) as Float with the ranges, units, and alarm limits specified. Create the three pressure-compensated Formula tags (FT_100_COMP, FT_101_COMP, MASS_BAL) using the equations in the control narrative. Write a User Program named 'DrumLevelControl' assigned to Background 1 at 100ms tick rate implementing the control algorithm from the narrative with the output clamped to 0-100%. On Page 1, build an operator display with numeric indicators for the four sensor tags, a bar graph for CV_100, and a trend viewer for MASS_BAL. Create a data logger named 'DrumLevelLog' per the narrative's logging section. Do NOT configure the DECOMMISSIONED tag TT_100. Save as boiler_drum_3element.c3 in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\. Login: Docker / GymAnything123!

metadata.standard: ASME PTC 4 / ISA-77.44.01 / NFPA 85 / NERC CIP
metadata.archetype_note: Requires tags + formula expressions + user program + display page + data logger. Agent must navigate 5 distinct UI sections with cross-referential dependencies.
